body_txt: So you're a badass who knows how to wield weapons, and have spare money to burn. What do you do? How about decorating those weapons lavishly ? Be it gold plating , gem encrusting , other sorts of exquisite knick-knacks, and even carving your name on them after that, the goal is to show people that these weapons are what you use when you mean business, that you're so good at the job that you make enough cash killing people to buy all this bling, or that you just like to add a "personal touch" to your weaponry. Or maybe to compensate for something else . Thankfully, most shows don't assume Viewers Are Morons by stating that a weapon is solid gold, as this would not be a very good idea due to the metal's relative softness. note The bullet, on the other hand, often is solid gold, and this is practical: Gold is heavier than lead, which many ordinary bullets are made of, and can be alloyed to make a hardened jacket. Silver weapons are seldom featured under this trope, since they're generally reserved for fighting supernatural creatures , plus the cultural view that silver is "second best". In video games (especially shooting games), expect a tricked-out weapon to be a special unlock, with or without increased power. A common variant in shooting games is an excessive amount of Gun Accessories . This trope can also be the effect of upgrading your weapon's stats . Compare Bling of War (for highly decorated armour or uniforms), Gold Makes Everything Shiny ( gold is a simple way to scream "fancy and expensive!"), Money Mauling (when your weapon is literally money), Gold-Colored Superiority , and Elegant Weapon for a More Civilized Age . May result from Elemental Crafting . Related to Gemstone Assault . Often overlaps with Elaborate Equals Effective . Sub-Trope of Cool, but Inefficient , Conspicuous Consumption , Made O' Gold . The more refined and less ostentatious examples may fall under Elegant Character, Elegant Weapon . This item is available in the Trope Co. catalog.
